input.command:
cat >> memory/findings.md << 'EOF'

## DAY 2026-03-28 — SIMULATION COMPLETE (all 330 resolved)
FINAL: TW-score=1245.77 | accuracy=20.9% | Brier skill=0.080 | 330/330 resolved.
Last 3: 144312=Macron (had Pope, -11.73); 26340=Kuwait (0.12#4, -13.92); 267975=White Nile (not listed -9.33).
TW trajectory: ~41(d1) -> 190 -> 459 -> 696 -> 967 -> 1085 -> peak ~1517(Mar6) -> 1246(final).

=== TOP LESSONS (for future runs) ===
1. BIGGEST WINS: cross-reference/structural inference applied EARLY on dominant clusters (Iran war, Gaddafi burial, SCOTUS Calif, Bangladesh, carrier, Grok). +40 to +78 each.
2. BIGGEST LOSSES: over-confidence (0.5-0.7) on the "OBVIOUS" answer w/o direct confirmation (Novichok, Hezbollah x3, Solberg, Chuck Norris-Texas, Telegram). Cap obvious <=0.40 unless direct-news-confirmed or airtight structural.
3. Balanced flat spreads (top<=0.25, 5 outcomes) on speculative/specific Qs net consistently small-POSITIVE when answer is in spread; minimal-mass on unguessable NAMES = ~abstain (correct).
4. Apply insight EARLY - earlier days dominate TW; late fixes barely help.
5. Synonym/format: answer the TYPE asked (person NAME not country; full ship/official designation; include official+common name variants e.g. IRIS Dena, Olmeca/Dos Bocas).
6. "which news org" -> Al Jazeera (resolution-source bias). Iran-US mediator -> Pakistan/Oman. Lebanon "which group" -> often al-Jamaa al-Islamiya/Hamas NOT Hezbollah.
7. War shifts "obvious": recipients/targets move toward war theater (Australia missiles->UAE; ambiguous casualty->Iran).
8. Specific war CITY Qs resolve OBSCURE (Minab/Qazvin/Bushehr/Qom not Tehran) -> wide flat spread.
EOF
echo "Simulation complete. Final TW=1245.77"
